reasoning: Initial observation suggests a linear relationship between absorbance and the product of molar absorptivity, concentration, and path length. To confirm this, I will increase the molar absorptivity and observe the change in absorbance.
The absorbance increased proportionally with the molar absorptivity, supporting the Beer-Lambert law. Next, I will increase the concentration to further validate the relationship.
The absorbance has doubled when both molar absorptivity and concentration were doubled, indicating a direct relationship. Increasing the path length will help confirm the full equation.

The absorbance has reached the observed value when all three variables (molar absorptivity, concentration, and path length) were appropriately scaled. This supports the Beer-Lambert law, which states that absorbance is proportional to the product of molar absorptivity, concentration, and path length.
